data.description: BUILDPLAN M3 / WS-8 (BUILDPLAN id MA-13). Orchestration tests with mocked MCP client plus mocked/cassette Gemini: verify (Clean/Broken/Stuck), commit handlers (commitOne, commitAll, revertOne, escalate - success and git-failure paths, no silent catch), finalSweep (Clean and Dirty), processIssues (happy path; retry-then-success; revert-on-persistent-failure); smoke tests for cli.ts and workflows/tidy-and-debug.ts. Tests target CURRENT behavior - the MA-15 refactor must keep them green. ACs: AC-19, AC-20. Depends: MA-10, MA-11. Size L.
data.title: Orchestration test coverage for detective core

data.body:
OWN-REVIEWER PROTOCOL — diff cfff009 vs origin/claude/ma-10-mcp-schemas (37c7fcd), 10 files, +1015/-129.

Verdict: APPROVE (1 Major found and fixed pre-push; 2 Minor accepted).

Findings:
1. [Major — FIXED] src/handlers/verify.ts:~60, commit.ts:~24, finalSweep.ts:~34 — new doc comments contained the literal underscore-underscore-definition token (3 hits in diff), which would trip AC-13's H-9 grep gate once scripts/debt-gates.sh lands. Reworded to 'underscore-private surface'; diff now has 0 hits.
2. [Minor — accepted] tests/cli.test.ts:12 pins node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs (tsx internal path) instead of the .bin shim. Stable for the locked tsx 4.x; revisit on a tsx major bump.
3. [Minor — accepted] tests/handlers/commit.test.ts:137 pins revertOne's deliberate exec_run swallow as CURRENT behavior (per ticket contract); if MA-15/WS-14 tightens that carve-out the test names the behavior to change.

Verified: handle-body extractions in verify/commit/finalSweep are hunk-by-hunk behavior-preserving (schemas hoisted unchanged, logic identical, exportNames untouched); tests use only public seams (module exports, makeProcessIssuesHandle, mocked mcp module whose fake still parses canned payloads through the real zod schemas); handler matching by object identity (MA-12-safe, no propose* internals asserted); no .only/.skip; no 'as any'/z.any() under src/handlers (AC-12); falcon-agent untouched (G-1). Gates: npm test 110/110 (~3.3s wall, max single test 2.4s < 5s, suite well under 60s); tsc --noEmit clean; biome clean. AC-19 scenarios all present; AC-20 both smokes present.

PR opened: https://github.com/mrojas54/maltese-agent/pull/43 (head cfff0090a68f1a84bf22b6831e42258b0373c5b7, base main, stacked on #40/MA-10 — merge #40 first). Gates at head: npm test 110/110 (~3.3s wall), tsc --noEmit clean, biome clean. Own-review attached in prior comment; worktree ma-13-orchestration-tests left intact for the orchestrator's fresh-eyes review roundtrip.

VERDICT: PASS

Reviewed origin/main...origin/claude/ma-13-orchestration-tests (head cfff009, matches expected). CI green: "Rust (fmt + clippy + test)" and "TypeScript (falcon-detective build + test)" both SUCCESS (gh pr checks 43). PR is OPEN/MERGEABLE against main.

(a) Enabling source change (verify.ts, commit.ts, finalSweep.ts) — SAFE, verified line-by-line:
Each `handle: async ({value}) => {...}` closure was lifted verbatim into a named exported `*Handle` function (verifyHandle, commitOneHandle, commitAllHandle, revertOneHandle, escalateHandle, finalSweepHandle) and `createHandler({..., handle: xHandle}, "name")` now references it by identifier. Confirmed: identical body (same mcp.connect/call/close-in-finally sequencing, same branching, same error propagation), identical inputValidator/outputValidator (schemas hoisted to named consts — e.g. CommitOneInput = McpAndPath.extend({issue, diffPath}) — byte-identical shape to the inline version), identical registration name string as createHandler's 2nd positional arg, identical exported const names (verify, commitOne, commitAll, revertOne, escalate, finalSweep) so import sites elsewhere are untouched. No control-flow, closure-capture, or engine-registration changes. This is a mechanical extract-function refactor.

(b) Tests assert real behavior, not tautologies:
- verify.test.ts (5 tests): Clean (asserts exact call sequence + args + client closed), Broken-via-cargo_check (asserts cargo_test never called), Broken-via-cargo_test, Stuck (asserts zero MCP instances spawned), and an explicit no-silent-catch test (cargo_check throws → rejects with message, client still closed). Would catch a broken verify (wrong kind returned, wrong short-circuit, swallowed error, unclosed client).
- commit.test.ts (8 tests): commitOne success (exact git_add/git_commit call+args+message format, sha returned), line-suffix omission variant, git_add failure and git_commit failure both propagate (no silent catch) while still closing; commitAll (marker only, zero MCP touch — correct, it's a no-op by design); revertOne success (exec_run args, enableExec:true, closed) and revertOne's exec_run failure — this one is a pinned *existing* swallow-by-design (`.catch(() => {})`, commented in source pre-PR as "no-op if file not modified"), and the test explicitly labels it "documented no-op-if-unmodified carve-out (CURRENT behavior)" rather than pretending it's a new silent-catch violation. Honest and correctly scoped. escalate (console.error content + no MCP touch). Would catch a broken commit message format, a broken revert exec invocation, or a genuinely new silent catch in commitOne.
- finalSweep.test.ts (3 tests): Clean (asserts context threaded through to value), Dirty (asserts reason strings and ordering: tests-then-clippy), no-silent-catch (cargo_test throws → rejects, client still closed).
- processIssues.test.ts (3 tests, via MA-11's pre-existing `makeProcessIssuesHandle(invoke)` injectable seam — NOT introduced by this PR, confirmed zero diff on processIssues.ts): happy path (exact handler-call sequence by object identity + exact commitOne input incl. diffPath), retry-then-success (asserts previousFailure payload carries prior diff+errors, attempt counter increments, exactly one in-loop revert then success — no terminal revert), revert-on-persistent-failure (3 Broken attempts, commitOne never invoked, 4 total fs_write reverts = 3 in-loop + 1 terminal, all restore original content). This is the strongest file in the PR — genuinely exercises the retry/revert state machine, not just happy path.
- cli.test.ts / tidy-and-debug.test.ts (AC-20 smoke): cli spawns the real entrypoint as a subprocess and asserts exit 1 + stderr on missing --target, verified hermetic (arg() throws before any console.log/engine work — confirmed by reading cli.ts). Workflow test flattens the Barnum Action AST and asserts stage order + branch arms by object identity against the public `@barnum/barnum/pipeline` Action shape — no private/underscore surface touched (satisfies AC-13's constraint referenced in the source comments).

(c) No reach into propose*-handler internals: processIssues.test.ts fakes the MA-11 invoke seam and matches by OBJECT IDENTITY against `proposeBugFix` as currently imported from `../../src/handlers/proposeBugFix.js`. Confirmed proposeBugFix.js is now a thin re-export from MA-12's `proposeFix.js` factory (`makeProposeFixHandler`) — identity survives the re-export, and the test comment explicitly documents "MA-12 may refactor propose* freely underneath this." No factory internals asserted on.

(d) Scenario coverage vs ticket checklist — complete, nothing missing:
verify Clean/Broken/Stuck ✓; commitOne/commitAll/revertOne/escalate success+git-failure, no-silent-catch (scoped honestly per-handler) ✓; finalSweep Clean/Dirty ✓; processIssues happy/retry-then-success/revert-on-persistent-failure ✓; cli.ts smoke ✓; workflows/tidy-and-debug.ts smoke ✓.

(e) Hermeticity: fully mocked FalconMcpClient (vi.mock of src/lib/mcp.js), fake invoke seam for processIssues — zero network, zero real git/API calls, no filesystem writes outside the fakes. CI vitest log (job 86400454968) confirms new files run in single-digit ms each: commit.test.ts 16ms/8 tests, verify.test.ts 14ms/5 tests, processIssues.test.ts 14ms/3 tests, finalSweep.test.ts 13ms/3 tests, tidy-and-debug.test.ts 8ms/3 tests; cli.test.ts is the one real subprocess spawn at 2221ms (still hermetic — fails during arg parsing before any pipeline/MCP/network work). 110 passed / 1 skipped, 22 files, full-suite Duration 59.28s.

MINOR/NIT (not blocking, not introduced by this PR): the 59.28s total is almost entirely pre-existing `tests/e2e.test.ts`'s cassette-miss skip check, which alone takes 58.35s just to determine "skip" — that's not touched by this diff (confirmed no diff to e2e.test.ts) but it means the suite is now sitting right at the ≤60s guardrail edge purely from pre-existing overhead, and MA-13's own additions (~2.3s, mostly cli.test.ts's subprocess) have zero margin left to work with. Worth a follow-up ticket to make the e2e cassette-miss check fail fast instead of ~58s, independent of MA-13.

(f) G-1 guardrail: confirmed zero changes to falcon-agent/** in this diff (`git diff --name-only` filtered on falcon-agent returned nothing).

FINDINGS SUMMARY:
- Critical: none
- Major: none
- Minor: none blocking
- NIT: pre-existing tests/e2e.test.ts cassette-miss check eats 58.3s of the 59.28s suite runtime, leaving the ≤60s guardrail with near-zero margin for future additions. Not caused by MA-13; recommend a separate fast-fail fix for e2e.test.ts's cassette-miss path.

RECOMMENDATION: MERGE. The enabling source change is a clean, verified behavior-preserving extract-function refactor (verified line-by-line against origin/main). Test coverage matches every ticket-listed scenario, assertions are substantive (call sequences, exact args, error propagation, client lifecycle, retry/revert state), no test reaches into MA-12's propose* factory internals, hermeticity is confirmed both by static read and by the actual CI vitest log, and CI is green on both Rust and TypeScript jobs. G-1 (no falcon-agent changes) holds.
Fresh-eyes review returned PASS / MERGE (posted above). PR #43 CI green. Merge attempt blocked by session permission layer — queued for operator alongside PR #46.
Merged (PR #43, merge commit 57524c8). CI green + independent fresh-eyes review PASS/MERGE (zero blocking findings; enabling named-function-export change verified mechanical).
